finding: FILL MODEL: contrarian entry after >=12bps prior move (microstructure family)

spread.got: n=1487 median 1c p95 2c p99 3c max 6c; reversal-family signal spreads n=71 median 1c p95 1c

adversarial_checks.fee_formula: feeEntry matches shares*0.07*p*(1-p) exactly on all 155 trades (max abs err $0.0000); total feeExit $0 — hold-to-resolution, no hedges/stops in family, no hidden double-fee legs
adversarial_checks.partial_fills: fillFrac = 1.0 on all 155 trades; no partial-fill flattery at $50 stakes (depth beyond $50/trade NOT established by this finding)
adversarial_checks.cap_discipline: 0 entries above 0.56, 0 asks above 0.55 — censoring exactly as described
adversarial_checks.censoring_attack_on_join: the -2.9c mean 'ask below minute-mid' IS partly a cap-censoring artifact: c20<=.54 subgroup (n=23) median -1.5c / mean -2.0c; c20>.54 subgroup (n=5, mechanically selected by the 55c cap) mean -6.9c. Honest uncensored fast-entry discount ~-1.5c median, not -2c. Direction survives; magnitude overstated ~0.5-1c on this secondary parameter
adversarial_checks.conservatism_direction: backtests using c20+1c fills with >55c skips are CONSERVATIVE vs live: join-set per-share EV at ledger fills -0.009 vs -0.049 at c20+1c fills; live fillability 85% > modeled 69% availability. The proposed cost model does not flatter strategies
adversarial_checks.edge_disclosure: finding correctly does NOT claim the reversal edge clears fees on TEST (last-third winrate .38-.39 < q* .5036); it is framed and only usable as a cost input

conclusion: Fill-distribution deliverable reproduces exactly and survives the fees/fills attack. One correction: replace the '-2c below minute-mid' fast-entry recipe with ~-1.5c (or use the ledger entry quantiles directly), because the join delta is censoring-inflated.
